description_long: This is a hand-crafted, ancient-style ceramic vase with a wide, slightly flared rim and a rounded, bulbous body tapering toward the base. The surface is uneven and textured, with visible signs of wear and age, including patches of discoloration and rough, matte areas. Subtle decorative elements are present, such as raised circular knobs and incised concentric arc patterns on the body. The coloration is a mix of gray, off-white, and pale brown, giving it a weathered, archaeological appearance. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding water or other contents, and the rim is thick and slightly irregular. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in an urban park, public square, or historical-themed garden.
description: A weathered, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a flared rim, rounded body, and subtle raised and incised decorations. Its surface is rough and aged, featuring gray and pale brown tones.
description_view_0: The vase is seen with two raised knobs and visible incised arc patterns on a rough, patchy surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights a single prominent knob and more pronounced discoloration near the rim and body.
description_view_2: The rounded body and two raised knobs are visible, with the incised arcs more clearly defined on the left side.
description_view_3: The surface shows the most extensive incised arc patterns and two knobs, with a large area of lighter discoloration.
